zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Carrington Smith於1370年出生在英國林可爾史爾韓東普時，他的父親John Carrington Smythe20歲，母親Anne Garvays20歲。他於1389年與Joan Willoughby在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1429年在英國諾廷加姆爾諾維爾享年59歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾克萊 可托。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Carrington Smith于1370年出生在英国林可爾史爾韓東普时，他的父亲John Carrington Smythe20岁，母亲Anne Garvays20岁。他于1389年与Joan Willoughby在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1429年在英国諾廷加姆爾諾維爾享年59岁，埋葬在英国諾薩普頓史爾克萊 可托。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Carrington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, a local name from any of several places in England so named. The one in Gloucestershire is an Old English compound meaning ‘settlement of Beorn's people’. In the Somerset place name the first element is an unattested Old English personal name Bāra, which also occurs, in the genitive form, as the first element of the Cambridgeshire place name. Barrington has also been used as an English form of Gaelic Ó Bearáin.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Carrington Smith was born in 1370, in Hanthorpe, Lincolnshire, England, his father, John Carrington Smythe, was 20 and his mother, Anne Garvays, was 20. He married Joan Willoughby in 1389, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1429, in Norwell, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Clay Coton, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Carrington Smith est né en 1370, à Hanthorpe, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, John Carrington Smythe, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Anne Garvays, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Joan Willoughby en 1389, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1429, à Norwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Clay Coton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Carrington Smith 1370 in Hanthorpe, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Carrington Smythe 20 und seine Mutter Anne Garvays 20. Er hat 1389 in England, United Kingdom Joan Willoughby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1429 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Norwell, Nottinghamshire, England und wurde in Clay Coton, Northamptonshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Carrington Smith è nato il nel 1370 nel Hanthorpe, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, John Carrington Smythe, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Anne Garvays, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Joan Willoughby nel 1389, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1429, nel Norwell, Nottinghamshire, England, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Clay Coton, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Carrington Smithが1370年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアハンソルペで生まれた時，父John Carrington Smytheは20歳，母Anne Garvaysは20歳であった。1389年にイギリスイギリスでJoan Willoughbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1429年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアノーウエルで59歳で死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアクレイ コトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Carrington Smith(이)가 1370년 영국 링코실르 핸소에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Carrington Smythe(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Garvays(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1389년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Joan Willoughby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1429년 영국 노틴그함실르 노웰에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 클레이 컷에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Carrington Smith nasceu em 1370, em Hanthorpe, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Carrington Smythe, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Anne Garvays, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Willoughby em 1389, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1429, em Norwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Clay Coton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Carrington Smith родился в 1370 г. в местности хантаурп, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Carrington Smythe было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Garvays, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Willoughby в 1389 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1429 г. в местности норвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности клеи котон, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Carrington Smith nació en 1370, en Hanthorpe, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Carrington Smythe, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Anne Garvays, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Joan Willoughby en 1389, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1429, en Norwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Clay Coton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
